title: Java Classes Should Not Be Empty
description: Empty classes should not be used. If a class is only being used as a common extension point, it should be replaced with an interface. If it was created as a placeholder for future development, it should be completed or removed. Exceptions include empty classes used as marker types, such as those annotated for Spring.
recommendation: Replace empty classes with interfaces or develop them further. For marker types, consider using annotations.
impact: Improves code readability and avoids unnecessary clutter in the code base.
